perturbation.original_text: Payment of the Invoice Total is due (without any right of set-off) Net forty-five (45) days after the date of invoice.
perturbation.changed_text: Payment of the Invoice Total is due (without any right of set-off) Net thirty (30) days after the date of invoice.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the payment terms. Initially, invoices were due Net 45 days. This change modifies the deadline to Net 30 days. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 7.2, which refers to the original 45-day period when discussing invoice disputes and partial payments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If Distributor disputes any invoice or part thereof, Distributor must notify Airspan in writing within twenty-five (25) days of the invoice date giving details of the reason for such dispute. Distributor and Airspan agree to work together in good faith to resolve the dispute as quickly as possible. Distributor may withhold payment of such disputed amounts until resolution of the dispute. Any parts of the invoice not under dispute shall be paid net forty-five (45) days after the date of invoice. On resolution of the dispute, Distributor shall immediately pay any amounts due in full.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline from 'Net forty-five (45) days' to 'Net thirty (30) days'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which explicitly states 'Any parts of the invoice not under dispute shall be paid net forty-five (45) days after the date of invoice.' The change creates a clear inconsistency regarding the payment deadline, making the terms unenforceable without clarification.

perturbation.original_text: All risk of loss or damage to the Equipment and Software will pass to Distributor on collection by the freight forwarder from Airspan. However, Airspan will insure the Equipment against loss or damage in transit to the agreed to location as set forth in the Purchase Order within the city limits of the arrival airport as set forth in Paragraph 8.1.
perturbation.changed_text: All risk of loss or damage to the Equipment and Software will pass to Distributor immediately after being loaded in the freight forwarder from Airspan. However, Airspan will not insure the Equipment against loss or damage in transit to the agreed to location as set forth in the Purchase Order within the city limits of the arrival airport as set forth in Paragraph 8.1.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the risk of loss passes to the Distributor when the freight forwarder collects the equipment from Airspan, but Airspan insures the equipment in transit. By changing the text to indicate that Airspan will not insure the equipment in transit, it creates a contradiction. This contradicts Section 14.2, where Airspan provides a warranty for the Equipment sold to Distributor under this Agreement shall, under normal use and service, be free from defects in materials and faulty workmanship.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to Sections 14.3 and 14.4, Airspan warrants that the Equipment sold to Distributor under this Agreement shall, under normal use and service, be free from defects in materials and faulty workmanship, and that the Software licensed to Distributor under this Agreement shall conform in all material respects to Airspan's published specifications therefor. The warranty period for any item of Equipment and related Software shall be twelve (12) months from the date of delivery of such Equipment and related Software to Distributor as set forth in Paragraph 8.1 (hereinafter, this period of time shall be referred to as the "Initial Warranty Period.")
perturbation.justification: The original text indicated that while the risk of loss shifted to the distributor upon collection by the freight forwarder, Airspan would insure the equipment during transit. The changed text negates Airspan's insurance responsibility during transit. Section 14.2 provides a warranty against defects in materials and faulty workmanship, implying Airspan's responsibility for the equipment's condition. By stating Airspan will not insure the equipment in transit, the changed text creates a contradiction with Section 14.2 because if the equipment is damaged in transit before delivery due to reasons other than defects in materials, the distributor's recourse under the warranty is unclear given the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: After the Initial Term, the term of the Agreement may be extended by a further period of five (5) years by mutual written agreement by the parties in the form of an amendment to this Agreement. If Distributor wishes to extend the term of the Agreement beyond the Initial Term, it must notify Airspan in writing at least six (6) months prior to then end of the Initial Term.
perturbation.changed_text: After the Initial Term, the term of the Agreement may be extended by a further period of one (1) year by mutual written agreement by the parties in the form of an amendment to this Agreement. If Distributor wishes to extend the term of the Agreement beyond the Initial Term, it must notify Airspan in writing at least two (2) months prior to then end of the Initial Term.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows for a five-year extension with a six-month notice. The modified text shortens the extension period to one year and the notice period to two months. This creates an in-text contradiction, because on Section 13.6. Airspan will recognize payments due to Distributor for orders received up to ninety (90) days after the termination or expiration of this Agreement. The text creates a contradiction here, because the initial term is a different one, with different notificaion, and different date for the extension, which makes it uncertain and can lead to disagreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Airspan shall not be liable to Distributor for damages of any kind, including incidental or consequential damages, on account of the termination of this agreement in accordance with this section 13. Airspan shall not be liable to Distributor on account of termination or expiration of this Agreement for reimbursement or damages for loss of goodwill, prospective profits, or anticipated orders, or on account of any expenditures, investments, leases, or commitments made by either party, or for any other reason whatsoever based upon or growing out of such termination or expiration. Airspan will recognize payments due to Distributor for orders received up to ninety (90) days after the termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the extension period from five years to one year and the notification period from six months to two months. This directly contradicts the contradicted text regarding payment for orders received up to ninety days after termination, creating an inconsistency regarding the effective termination date and related payment obligations. The Airspan shall not be liable for loss of goodwill, prospective profits, or anticipated orders, or on account of any expenditures, investments, leases, or commitments made by either party based upon or growing out of such termination or expiration, directly contradicting the payment terms after the termination or expiration of the Agreement. This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' since 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.'
